Operation 'receivers.create' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context. Description is too brief and lacks context about required inputs and outputs. AI agents need to understand what parameters are required, what the operation returns, and the receiver's role in the payment method distribution workflow. Enhanced description provides input expectations, authentication requirements, and output details.

data.current_description: Create a receiver for use in [payment method distribution](https://docs.spreedly.com/guides/payment-method-distribution/).
data.description_suggestion: Create a new payment method distribution receiver. Requires authentication and receiver configuration details (name, receiver type, and credentials). Returns the created receiver object with a unique token for subsequent operations. The receiver will be used to distribute payment methods to external systems.
data.reasoning: Description is too brief and lacks context about required inputs and outputs. AI agents need to understand what parameters are required, what the operation returns, and the receiver's role in the payment method distribution workflow. Enhanced description provides input expectations, authentication requirements, and output details.

message: Operation 'receivers.show' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Description is minimal and doesn't explain what information is returned or authentication requirements. AI agents need to understand the response structure and prerequisites. Enhanced description clarifies the return value and authentication context.

data.current_description: Get a receiver with the given token.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve a specific receiver by its token. Returns the receiver object with all configuration details and current credentials. Requires authentication and a valid receiver token as a path parameter.
data.reasoning: Description is minimal and doesn't explain what information is returned or authentication requirements. AI agents need to understand the response structure and prerequisites. Enhanced description clarifies the return value and authentication context.

message: Operation 'receivers.update' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Description is too vague about what can be updated and what the operation returns. AI agents need clarity on input requirements (what fields can be updated), authentication, and the response structure. Enhanced description specifies credentials as the primary update target, clarifies the request/response structure, and explains the operational context.

data.current_description: Update a receiver with new credentials.
data.description_suggestion: Update receiver credentials and configuration. Requires authentication, a valid receiver token, and new credential values in the request body. Returns the updated receiver object. This operation allows you to refresh or modify the credentials used for payment method distribution to the receiver.
data.reasoning: Description is too vague about what can be updated and what the operation returns. AI agents need clarity on input requirements (what fields can be updated), authentication, and the response structure. Enhanced description specifies credentials as the primary update target, clarifies the request/response structure, and explains the operational context.

message: Operation 'receivers.redact' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Description explains what redaction does but lacks clarity on side effects and return value. AI agents need to understand that this is a destructive operation with permanent consequences (credentials removed, receiver deactivated) and what the response contains. Enhanced description clarifies the permanent nature of the operation, its effects on receiver state, and the response structure.

data.current_description: Redact (strip of any sensitive credentials and make inactive) a receiver.
data.description_suggestion: Redact a receiver by removing sensitive credentials and deactivating it. Requires authentication and a valid receiver token. This operation permanently removes all stored credentials from the receiver and marks it as inactive, preventing further use in payment method distribution. Returns the redacted receiver object with credentials removed.
data.reasoning: Description explains what redaction does but lacks clarity on side effects and return value. AI agents need to understand that this is a destructive operation with permanent consequences (credentials removed, receiver deactivated) and what the response contains. Enhanced description clarifies the permanent nature of the operation, its effects on receiver state, and the response structure.

message: Operation 'payment_methods.recache' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, unclear_purpose, missing_context. Original summary 'Recache' is too vague and uses internal terminology that doesn't convey the operation's purpose to AI agents. Description mentions only credit cards but should be more general for payment methods. Added clarity about the operation's purpose (enabling transactions), what it does (refreshes verification), and required inputs for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Update a credit card's verification value (CVV) so the card can be transacted against.
data.description_suggestion: Update a payment method's verification value (CVV) to enable it for transactions. This operation refreshes the card's verification credentials, allowing it to be used for subsequent transaction processing. Requires the payment method token as input.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Recache' is too vague and uses internal terminology that doesn't convey the operation's purpose to AI agents. Description mentions only credit cards but should be more general for payment methods. Added clarity about the operation's purpose (enabling transactions), what it does (refreshes verification), and required inputs for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description: Get an [ordered and paginated](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#order-and-pagination) list of transactions executed against a specific payment method.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve an ordered and paginated list of all transactions executed against a specific payment method. Supports filtering and sorting options. Returns transaction records with details about each operation performed using this payment method.

message: Operation 'certificates.create' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is adequate. Description provides good context about Spreedly's role but lacks clarity about what is returned and the workflow. Added explicit information about return values (certificate token, CSR content), the relationship to the update operation, and the expected workflow for AI agents to understand the complete operation lifecycle.

data.current_description:
Create a new SSL keypair and certificate signing request (CSR).

Since Spreedly is not a certificate authority we cannot issue trusted certificates. Instead, it is intended that you will get the certificate signed by your certificate authority (or sign it yourself) and update the certificate record at Spreedly with the final and signed cert.
data.description_suggestion: Create a new SSL keypair and certificate signing request (CSR). This operation generates a new certificate record with an unsigned CSR that must be signed by your certificate authority or self-signed. Returns the certificate token and CSR content. The certificate record can be updated later with the signed certificate using the update operation. Note: Spreedly does not issue trusted certificates.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description provides good context about Spreedly's role but lacks clarity about what is returned and the workflow. Added explicit information about return values (certificate token, CSR content), the relationship to the update operation, and the expected workflow for AI agents to understand the complete operation lifecycle.

message: Operation 'transactions.list' has semantic quality issues: missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary is adequate. Description includes important rate-limiting information but lacks clarity about return value structure and what types of transactions are included. Added explicit information about transaction record details, filtering/sorting support, and context about transaction types (local actions and payment processing) to improve AI agent understanding of what data will be returned.

data.current_description:
Retrieve an [ordered and paginated](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#order-and-pagination) list of all transactions in the authenticated environment.

Note: This endpoint is rate-limited to 30 requests per minute, per environment.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieve an ordered and paginated list of all transactions in the authenticated environment. Returns transaction records with details about each operation performed. Supports filtering and sorting options. Rate-limited to 30 requests per minute per environment. Each transaction captures local actions and payment processing operations.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate. Description includes important rate-limiting information but lacks clarity about return value structure and what types of transactions are included. Added explicit information about transaction record details, filtering/sorting support, and context about transaction types (local actions and payment processing) to improve AI agent understanding of what data will be returned.

message: Operation 'transactions.credit' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'Credit (refund)' is vague and uses parenthetical clarification. Description is poorly formatted with unclear structure. Missing information about what is returned and the side effect on transaction balance. Improved version clarifies the two refund scenarios, specifies return value, and explains the state change to the original transaction.

data.current_description:
Full amount: Refund the full amount of a purchase.

The request body may be omitted, or the `amount` field may be omitted from the request transaction.

Partial amount: Refund a partial amount of a purchase by specifying an `amount`.

The request body must be present.
data.summary_suggestion: Refund a transaction (full or partial amount)
data.description_suggestion: Refund a purchase transaction, either the full amount or a partial amount. For full refunds, omit the request body or omit the `amount` field. For partial refunds, specify the `amount` field in the request body. Returns a transaction object representing the credit/refund. The original transaction's available balance is reduced by the refunded amount.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Credit (refund)' is vague and uses parenthetical clarification. Description is poorly formatted with unclear structure. Missing information about what is returned and the side effect on transaction balance. Improved version clarifies the two refund scenarios, specifies return value, and explains the state change to the original transaction.

message: Operation 'gateways.general_credit' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'General credit' is too vague and doesn't clarify the action. Description is verbose and uses external link reference for context. Missing explicit return value information. Improved version clarifies the distinction from regular credit, specifies the return value, and consolidates gateway support information more concisely for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Add funds to a credit card outside the scope of a previous reference transaction. This is different from [credit](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#credit) which refunds money only up to the amount that had been taken during a previous transaction.

Support for this general credit depends on the gateway.
data.summary_suggestion: Add funds to a credit card (general credit)
data.description_suggestion: Add funds to a credit card outside the scope of a previous transaction. Unlike the credit operation which refunds up to a previously charged amount, general credit allows arbitrary fund additions. Returns a transaction object representing the credit. Gateway support for this operation varies by payment processor.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'General credit' is too vague and doesn't clarify the action. Description is verbose and uses external link reference for context. Missing explicit return value information. Improved version clarifies the distinction from regular credit, specifies the return value, and consolidates gateway support information more concisely for AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'gateways.verify' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'Verify' is too generic and doesn't specify what is being verified. Description relies on external link for key context and doesn't clearly state return value or side effects. Improved version specifies the verification target, clarifies the optional retention behavior, explains the return value, and documents the side effect of payment method retention.

data.current_description: Determine if a credit card is [chargeable card and available for purchases](https://docs.spreedly.com/guides/using-payment-methods/#verifying-a-card). The `retain_on_success` request parameter will automatically retain the card if it's successfully verified.
data.description_suggestion: Verify that a credit card is valid and available for purchases. Optionally set `retain_on_success` to true to automatically retain the payment method if verification succeeds. Returns a transaction object indicating verification success or failure. If successful and `retain_on_success` is true, the payment method is retained in the system.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Verify' is too generic and doesn't specify what is being verified. Description relies on external link for key context and doesn't clearly state return value or side effects. Improved version specifies the verification target, clarifies the optional retention behavior, explains the return value, and documents the side effect of payment method retention.

data.current_description: Deliver a payment method to the specified receiver endpoint. See the guide on [payment method distribution](https://docs.spreedly.com/guides/payment-method-distribution/) to understand how to specify what is sent to the receiver.
data.description_suggestion: Deliver a payment method to the configured receiver endpoint. The request specifies which payment method to deliver and optionally what data to include in the delivery. Returns a transaction object indicating delivery success or failure. The payment method data is transmitted to the receiver's endpoint according to the receiver's configuration.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Deliver' is too vague and doesn't indicate what is being delivered. Description relies on external guide for critical context about specifying delivery parameters. Missing information about return value and what happens during delivery. Improved version clarifies the delivery target, explains input parameters, specifies return value, and describes the operation's effect.

data.current_description: Export multiple payment methods in a single, asynchronous, batch call to an SFTP receiver endpoint.
data.summary_suggestion: Batch export payment methods to SFTP
data.description_suggestion: Asynchronously export multiple payment methods in a batch operation to an SFTP receiver endpoint. The request specifies which payment methods to export. Returns a transaction object representing the export job. The export is processed asynchronously and payment method data is delivered to the receiver's SFTP endpoint according to the receiver's configuration.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Export' is too generic and doesn't indicate the batch nature or SFTP destination. Description is incomplete - missing information about what is returned and how the asynchronous processing works. Improved version clarifies batch processing, specifies return value, explains asynchronous nature, and indicates where data is delivered.

message: Operation 'gateways.store' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but could be more concise and structured for AI agent clarity. Enhanced description to better organize the key information: what the operation does, what is returned (transaction with ThirdPartyToken), and critical constraints (gateway-specific, independent lifecycle management) for improved AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description:
Tokenize a payment method *at the gateway*.

This transaction copies the payment method information from the Spreedly vault [to the gateway's vault](https://docs.spreedly.com/guides/third-party-vaulting/) and creates a new Spreedly payment method to represent the gateway's version. The reference to the payment method at the gateway is called a `ThirdPartyToken` and is a separate payment method from the original payment method. It is up to you to manage the lifecycle of these linked payment methods – Spreedly does not keep them in sync in any way.

The `ThirdPartyToken` is locked to the gateway where the card is stored; it cannot be used at another gateway. If you'd like a card to be used at multiple gateways, you'll need to [retain the card in the Spreedly vault](https://docs.spreedly.com/basics/purchase/#retain).
data.description_suggestion: Tokenize a payment method at the specified gateway. This operation copies the payment method from the Spreedly vault to the gateway's vault and creates a new ThirdPartyToken payment method representing the gateway's stored version. The ThirdPartyToken is gateway-specific and cannot be used at other gateways. Returns a transaction object containing the new ThirdPartyToken payment method. Note: The original and gateway payment methods are managed independently and not kept in sync.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and action-oriented. Description is comprehensive but could be more concise and structured for AI agent clarity. Enhanced description to better organize the key information: what the operation does, what is returned (transaction with ThirdPartyToken), and critical constraints (gateway-specific, independent lifecycle management) for improved AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Create (provision) a gateway to process card data in the authenticated environment. A test gateway is used to [test your integration](https://docs.spreedly.com/guides/testing/) to Spreedly and can only be used with [test payment methods](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/test-data/). Production gateways process real card data and transactions. Production gateways are created using the credentials required for that gateway, meaning each call is slightly different depending on the gateway's API requirements.

data.current_description: When gateways are created via the API, they are automatically retained. However, gateways created via the unauthenticated API (e.g., from a browser/javascript library) are created in the "cached" state. Only by retaining a cached gateway will it be saved and available for future use.
data.summary_suggestion: Retain cached gateway for persistent use
data.description_suggestion: Retain a cached gateway to save it permanently and make it available for future transactions. Gateways created via the authenticated API are automatically retained, but gateways created via the unauthenticated API are initially in a cached state. Calling this operation persists the gateway and transitions it from cached to retained state. Requires the gateway_token path parameter. Returns the retained gateway object.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Retain' is too vague and doesn't indicate the action or its purpose. Description explains context but lacks clarity on what the operation does, its side effects, and return value. The transcript is automatically scrubbed of sensitive payment information, making it safe to share with external support representatives for debugging and troubleshooting. Note: Transcript format is not machine-parseable and may change without warning.

message: Operation 'gateways.purchase' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary 'Purchase' is too generic. Description is comprehensive but could be better structured for AI agent clarity. Improved summary to specify the action and target. Reorganized description to clearly distinguish input modes, specify required fields, and clarify the immediate fund transfer effect.

data.current_description:
Charge a payment method a specific amount at the target gateway.


Tokenized payment method: Charge a _tokenized_ payment method (already stored in the Spreedly environment) the specified amount. The payment method can be of any type (credit card, bank account, Apple Pay, etc…), as long as it exists in the specified environment.

The `payment_method_token` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in credit card: Pass a credit card payment method directly in to the purchase request. If the card is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `credit_card` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in bank account/eCheck: Pass a bank account/eCheck payment method directly in to the purchase request. If the bank account is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `bank_account` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Android Pay: Pass an Android Pay payment method directly in to the purchase request. If the Android Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `android_pay` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Google Pay: Pass a Google Pay payment method directly in to the purchase request. If the Google Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `google_pay` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Apple Pay: Pass an Apple Pay payment method directly in to the purchase request. If the Apple Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `apple_pay` field of the transaction request is required.
data.description_suggestion: Executes a purchase transaction by charging a payment method a specified amount at the target gateway. Supports two input modes: (1) Tokenized payment method - charge an existing payment method already stored in Spreedly (requires payment_method_token); (2) Pass-in payment method - provide payment details directly (credit card, bank account, Apple Pay, Google Pay, or Android Pay) which will be automatically tokenized before sending to gateway. Returns a transaction object with status, authorization code, and gateway response. Funds are immediately charged upon successful authorization.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Purchase' is too generic. Description is comprehensive but could be better structured for AI agent clarity. Improved summary to specify the action and target. Reorganized description to clearly distinguish input modes, specify required fields, and clarify the immediate fund transfer effect.

Useful for recurring charges or follow-up purchases with the same payment method.

message: Operation 'gateways.authorize' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary 'Authorize' is too generic and doesn't indicate the action or target. Description is comprehensive and well-structured with clear input modes and requirements. Only summary needs improvement to be more action-oriented and specific.

data.current_description:
Authorize a payment method to be charged a specific amount at the target gateway. No funds are taken with an authorize – a follow-up [capture](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#capture) transaction is required to actually move the funds.

The referenced gateway must exist in the environment whose environment key is being used to authenticate the API call.


Tokenized payment method: Authorize a _tokenized_ payment method (already stored in the Spreedly environment) to be charged a specific amount.

The `payment_method_token` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in credit card: Pass a credit card payment method directly in to the authorize request. If the card is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `credit_card` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Android Pay: Pass an Android Pay payment method directly in to the authorize request. If the Android Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `android_pay` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Google Pay: Pass a Google Pay payment method directly in to the authorize request. If the Google Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `google_pay` field of the transaction request is required.


Pass-in Apple Pay: Pass an Apple Pay payment method directly in to the authorize request. If the Apple Pay data is valid, it will automatically be tokenized at Spreedly before sending to the gateway.

The `apple_pay` field of the transaction request is required.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Authorize' is too generic and doesn't indicate the action or target. Description is comprehensive and well-structured with clear input modes and requirements. Only summary needs improvement to be more action-oriented and specific.

message: Operation 'transactions.capture' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context. Summary 'Capture' is too vague. Description is clear about modes but could be better structured for AI agent clarity. Improved summary to indicate the action and what is being captured. Reorganized description to clarify input modes, specify return value, and explain the settlement effect.

data.current_description:
Full amount: Capture all funds previously reserved via an [authorize](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#authorize).

The request body may be omitted, or the `amount` field may be omitted from the request transaction.

Partial amount: Capture a partial amount from an [authorize](https://docs.spreedly.com/reference/api/v1/?json#authorize) by specifying an `amount`.

The request body must be present.
data.description_suggestion: Captures funds previously reserved by an authorize transaction. Supports two modes: (1) Full capture - omit the amount field to capture the entire authorized amount; (2) Partial capture - specify an amount to capture less than the full authorization. Returns a transaction object with capture status and amount captured. Captured funds are settled to the merchant account. Must reference a previous authorize transaction token.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Capture' is too vague. Description is clear about modes but could be better structured for AI agent clarity. Improved summary to indicate the action and what is being captured. Reorganized description to clarify input modes, specify return value, and explain the settlement effect.

message: Operation 'transactions.void' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Summary 'Void' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what happens when voiding and when it can be used. Improved summary to be more descriptive. Enhanced description to explain the effect of voiding, applicable transaction types, return value, and practical use cases for AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Cancel an `authorization` transaction.

This API call can also be used to cancel a `capture`/`purchase` transaction that hasn't yet settled at the merchant account.
data.description_suggestion: Cancels a transaction, preventing funds from being charged or settled. Can void authorize transactions (canceling the authorization hold) or capture/purchase transactions that haven't yet settled at the merchant account. Returns a transaction object with void status. Once voided, a transaction cannot be captured or settled. Useful for canceling orders or correcting transaction errors before settlement.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Void' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what happens when voiding and when it can be used. Improved summary to be more descriptive. Enhanced description to explain the effect of voiding, applicable transaction types, return value, and practical use cases for AI agent understanding.
